zh.Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪可洛馬迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Urquhart 4th of Cromarty於大約1390年出生在蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪時，他的父親John Urquhart 3rd of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty23歲，母親Lady Agnes Ramsay of Dalhousie20歲。他於大約1440年與Susanna-nic-Alexander Forbes of Forbes在蘇格蘭可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1475年9月8日享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Urquhart 4th of Cromarty于大约1390年出生在苏格兰可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪时，他的父亲John Urquhart 3rd of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty23岁，母亲Lady Agnes Ramsay of Dalhousie20岁。他于大约1440年与Susanna-nic-Alexander Forbes of Forbes在苏格兰可洛馬迪夏爾可洛馬迪结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1475年9月8日享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Urquhart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Ross and Cromarty): habitational name from any of various places in Scotland so called, notably the barony of Urquhart (Inverness), or places in Ross and Cromarty, Morayshire, and Fife. All are thought to derive from Pictish ar ‘on, by’ + Pictish carden ‘enclosure’ or ‘wood, copse’. Some bearers of this name trace their descent from William de Urchard, who fought for Robert the Bruce between 1297 and 1328.

variants.name: Orcutt
meanings: English (Staffordshire): perhaps a much altered form of Scottish   Urquhart  .

variants.name: Orchard
meanings: topographic or occupational name from residence near (or perhaps employment at) an orchard, Middle English orchard (Old English ortgeard, orceard).

habitational name from any of the places called Orchard. Those in Devon and Somerset are named from Old English ortgeard, orceard (a compound of wort, wyrt ‘plant’ (later associated with Latin hortus ‘garden’) + geard ‘yard, enclosure’), while East and West Orchard near Shaftesbury in Dorset derive from Primitive Welsh ar ‘beside, in front of, facing’ + ced ‘wood’.
rare variant of Scottish   Urquhart  . Compare   Orahood  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Urquhart 4th of Cromarty was born about 1390, in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland, his father, John Urquhart 3rd of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty, was 23 and his mother, Lady Agnes Ramsay of Dalhousie, was 20. He married Susanna-nic-Alexander Forbes of Forbes about 1440, in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 8 September 1475, at the age of 86.

Il a épousé Susanna-nic-Alexander Forbes of Forbes environ 1440, à Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1475, à l’âge de 86 ans.

Er hat um 1440 in Cromarty, Cromartyshire, Scotland Susanna-nic-Alexander Forbes of Forbes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 8. September 1475 im Alter von 86 Jahren.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Urquhart 4th of Cromarty родился около 1390 г. в местности кромерти, кромертишир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Urquhart 3rd of Cromarty, Sheriff of Cromarty было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Ramsay of Dalhousie, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Susanna-nic-Alexander Forbes of Forbes около 1440 г. в местности кромерти, кромертишир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 8 сентября 1475 г. в возрасте 86 лет.
